food.name: Salmon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g edible-flesh basis keeps the entry aligned with a base whole-food ingredient rather than a cooked preparation.

scoreReadiness.summary: Strong enough for launch scripting as a raw salmon baseline, but a few protein-quality style fields are still legacy calibration proxies and salmon source variation can move the fat profile.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
wild-versus-farmed and species variation can shift the exact fat and omega-3 profile meaningfully

sourceNotes: Identity locked to raw salmon flesh, using Atlantic salmon style per-100 g baseline values.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style raw Atlantic salmon entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked salmon values.

sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 13g of fat. Omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
sections.subtitleText: 13g of fat. Omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 0g of carbs. The rest is not doing much. In this category, carbs barely matter.
sections.subtitleText: 0g of carbs. The rest is not doing much. In this category, carbs barely matter.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 20.4g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
sections.subtitleText: 20.4g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: Vitamin b12 at 117% daily value, vitamin d at 66% daily value stand out most. That is major vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: Vitamin b12 at 117% daily value, vitamin d at 66% daily value stand out most. That is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: Potassium at 8% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: Potassium at 8% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: Pros first: easy to build a strong meal around. It works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses. Very satisfying as a protein source. That makes it practical in real meals. Has unusually strong trust with viewers. People already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: The drawbacks next: can be expensive. Price can make it less practical. Quality varies with sourcing. Sourcing changes the real-world quality. Needs good storage and handling. It is less convenient than shelf-stable options.

displayItems.title: quality varies with sourcing
displayItems.explanation: Wild vs farmed differences matter in practice.

displayItems.title: needs good storage and handling
displayItems.explanation: A practical downside compared with some shelf-stable foods.

closing.summary: Big strengths are omega 3 and protein, but the biggest weaknesses are expensive and quality varies with sourcing.
closing.overview: Big strengths are omega 3 and protein, but the biggest weaknesses are expensive and quality varies with sourcing.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Vitamins are carrying this food most, while minerals are holding it back most. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 56.8. Section scores: fats 80, proteins 60, vitamins 84, minerals 0, pros 66.7, cons 50.
